metadata.official_name: Columbia University in the City of New York

strategic_profile.executive_summary: Columbia University is a highly elite, private Ivy League research university located in New York City. Renowned for its rigorous Core Curriculum and unparalleled access to the professional and cultural resources of Manhattan, it offers undergraduates a deeply intellectual and globally connected college experience.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The Core Curriculum is the defining feature of Columbia College, requiring all undergraduates to take a shared sequence of discussion-based seminars in literature, philosophy, art, and science.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must explicitly demonstrate an intellectual excitement for interdisciplinary, discussion-based learning and engaging with foundational texts.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Columbia maintains a permanent test-optional policy but still evaluates applicants with immense academic rigor, typically requiring students to be in the top 10% of their graduating class.
analyst_takeaways.implication: With a sub-4% regular decision acceptance rate, a rigorous transcript (AP/IB/Honors) is an absolute baseline; the test-optional policy shifts more weight to essays, recommendations, and demonstrated intellectual vitality.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Columbia is need-blind for all domestic and undocumented applicants, meeting 100% of demonstrated financial need with no-loan packages.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students from lower and middle-income families (under $150,000) often attend tuition-free, making it highly accessible despite a daunting sticker price.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A tightly knit, driven, and intellectually vibrant campus set against the backdrop of New York City. Social life often revolves around NYC's cultural offerings and a local bar culture (Mel's, 1020, The Heights) rather than traditional college sports.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in Morningside Heights with immediate subway access, the city serves as an extended campus, providing seamless access to top-tier internships, museums, and Wall Street.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Columbia hosts hundreds of labs and institutes. The Undergraduate Research Opportunities (UROP) portal allows students to easily connect with cutting-edge projects across the Morningside Campus and the Irving Medical Center.

current_status.test_policy_details: Permanent test-optional policy. Standardized test scores are 'Considered' rather than 'Very Important'. Superscores both the SAT and ACT.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data omitted/blank in official CDS releases.

gpa.notes: Columbia does not publish an average GPA, but over 95% of admitted students rank in the top 10% of their graduating class.

testing.policy_note: Score submission is permanently optional. Superscores both SAT and ACT.

colleges.name: Columbia College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students with a deep intellectual curiosity across disciplines, who are genuinely excited by the prospect of the humanities-driven Core Curriculum and analyzing foundational texts alongside peers.
colleges.housing_profile: Guaranteed on-campus housing for all four years.
colleges.student_archetype: Intellectually voracious, well-rounded thinkers eager to participate in rigorous Socratic dialogue.

majors.name: Computer Science
prerequisite_courses: ENGI E1006 INTRO TO COMP FOR ENG/APP SCI
COMS W1004 Intro to Computer Science and Programming in Java
majors.special_requirements: Requires completion of specific math sequences including Calculus III or Multivariable Calculus, plus Linear Algebra and Probability/Statistics.

core_courses: COMS W1004 Intro to Computer Science and Programming in Java
COMS W3134 Data Structures in Java
CSEE W3827 Fundamentals of Computer Systems
electives: BMCS E4480 Statistical machine learning for genomics

majors.notes: Up to four transfer courses can be accepted toward the major. Calculus, linear algebra, and probability/statistics can be transferred in addition to the four-course limit.

majors.name: Economics
majors.special_requirements: Must receive a grade of C- or higher in any class taken for major credit (except UN1105 taken Pass/D/Fail).
majors.notes: Barnard Economics Core Courses and seminars do NOT count towards the Columbia Economics Major.

majors.name: Political Science
majors.special_requirements: Students must choose a primary subfield and a secondary subfield from American Politics, Comparative Politics, International Relations, or Political Theory.

core_courses: POLS UN2201 Introduction to American Politics
POLS UN2501 Introduction to Comparative Politics

majors.notes: Courses in the Core Curriculum do not fulfill requirements for the Political Science major.

colleges.name: Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science (SEAS)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students with extraordinary STEM aptitude who also value a strong humanities foundation, as SEAS students must complete a specialized engineering liberal arts core.
colleges.housing_profile: Guaranteed on-campus housing for all four years.
colleges.student_archetype: Driven innovators and problem solvers eager to leverage cutting-edge technology for societal impact.

majors.name: Computer Science
prerequisite_courses: ENGI E1006 INTRO TO COMP FOR ENG/APP SCI

core_courses: COMS W1004 Intro to Computer Science and Programming in Java
COMS W3134 Data Structures in Java
CSEE W3827 Fundamentals of Computer Systems

majors.notes: Shared curriculum structure with Columbia College CS majors but fulfills SEAS specific core requirements.

application_deadlines.notes: Binding Early Decision. Admissions and financial aid decisions released in mid-December.
Regular Decision deadline. Decisions released late March.

supplemental_requirements.details: Includes a list question (100-word limit) about media/books, and five 150-word short essays addressing topics such as 'Why Columbia', 'Lived Experience', and 'Why Major'.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Columbia completely eliminated alumni interviews in 2023. Unlike many peer institutions, standardized test scores are labeled 'Considered' rather than 'Very Important' due to their permanent test-optional policy.

major_selection_tactics: Major selection on the application is largely inconsequential for Columbia College admissions, as all students enter undeclared and do not formally declare a major until sophomore year.
Attempting to 'game' the system by selecting an unpopular major does not provide an admissions advantage.
Applicants to SEAS must show a strong math and science background, distinguishing them from Columbia College candidates.
college_ranking_tactics: Applying Early Decision yields a significant mathematical advantage (approx. 11-13% acceptance rate vs. sub-3% for Regular Decision) as admissions officers factor in guaranteed yield.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Does not apply; Columbia does not evaluate undergraduate admissions strictly by intended major capacity (with the exception of distinct differences between Columbia College and SEAS).

financials.tuition_model: Flat tuition charge for full-time Columbia College and SEAS students.

scholarships.name: Columbia Grant
scholarships.benefits: Covers 100% of demonstrated need without loans. Families earning under $150,000 typically attend tuition-free.

scholarships.name: Start-up Grant
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to incoming first-year students from low-income families to help ease their transition to college.

ap_policy.general_rule: Grants credit or advanced standing for scores of 4 or 5 depending on the department. Maximum of 16 points of pre-matriculation credit can be applied.
exceptions: AP Economics requires tests in both Micro and Macro, with a 5 in one and at least a 4 in the other for exemption.
AP Calculus BC score of 5 yields 6 credits.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used for course exemption or to fulfill specific prerequisites, but cannot exceed 16 credits total.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants up to 6 points of credit for Higher Level (HL) exam grades of 6 or 7 in appropriate disciplines.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer applicants must have completed 24 points of credit (one year of full-time study) at another institution. Minimum recommended GPA of 3.5. Internal transfers between Columbia College and SEAS are allowed but require a formal application by March 1 and are not guaranteed.

what_it_takes: Exceptional academic rigor taking the hardest courses available (AP/IB/Honors).
Authentic intellectual curiosity and a genuine enthusiasm for the Core Curriculum.
Depth over breadth in extracurriculars, showing leadership and tangible impact.
A coherent intellectual narrative that connects passions to specific Columbia resources.
common_activities: Sustained community service or social justice advocacy
Deeply involved artistic or musical pursuits
essay_tips: Be highly specific in the 'Why Columbia' essay—mention particular texts in the Core or distinct qualities of discussion-based learning.
In the 'Lived Experience' prompt, explicitly connect your identity/background to how you will contribute to Columbia's shared intellectual environment.
Avoid generalizing about wanting to live in New York City or attending an Ivy League school.
red_flags: Superficial list of clubs without demonstrated leadership or impact.
Writing about New York City as the primary reason for applying, rather than academic fit.
Generic responses that do not mention the Core Curriculum or specific faculty/offerings.
insights: Columbia's urban location means social life often defers to NYC's cultural and bar scenes rather than campus-contained sports.
The 'weeder' culture in STEM classes (like Organic Chemistry and Econometrics) is notable, but grade deflation is not as severe as rumored.
There is no demonstrated interest tracked, meaning campus visits won't boost admissions odds.
